title: rosa canina subvar. oblonga
intro: Rosa canina subvar. oblonga, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa canina plant, native to Europe and western Asia. The medicinal properties of Rosa canina subvar. oblonga are attributed to its high content of vitamins C and E, as well as its antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and reduce inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for colds, fever, and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Rosa canina subvar. oblonga include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal products, such as rosehip oil and rose petal jelly.
